name: hero-photo-section
role: Photo-led marketing hero. Hero category 4 of 5. Anchors the canonical hero anatomy (eyebrow + headline + subhead + actions + meta) against a brand photographic image. Four layouts — image-right (50/50 split, default), image-left (mirrored), image-full-bleed (image fills hero, text overlays), image-overlay (text on dark/light tinted image). Responsive image handling via <picture> with srcset + media queries for art direction. Production-grade LCP optimization via fetchpriority='high' on the hero image.

layouts.image-right: 50/50 split — text on left column, image on right column. The 80% choice. Mobile breakpoint collapses to image-below.
layouts.image-left: Mirrored — image on left, text on right. Used for art-direction variety.
layouts.image-full-bleed: Image fills the hero section corner-to-corner. Text overlays in a positioned content column (typically left-anchored). Used on flagship marketing home.
layouts.image-overlay: Same as full-bleed but with a deliberate overlay (gradient or tint) applied to image for guaranteed text contrast. Used when text must stay legible against unpredictable image content.

lcpOptimization.rule: The hero image is the LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) candidate for marketing pages. Optimize aggressively.
lcpOptimization.fetchpriority: on the hero image. Tells the browser to prioritize this resource over other images.
lcpOptimization.lazyLoadingDisabled: Do NOT set loading='lazy' on the hero image — that's the opposite of what you want. Hero is above-fold.
lcpOptimization.responsiveSizes: Use <picture> with <source media='...' srcset='...'> for art direction at different breakpoints. Different crops for mobile vs desktop.
lcpOptimization.modernFormats: Prefer AVIF + WebP source sets with JPEG/PNG fallback. first, next, fallback.

meta.licenseNote: Photographic images are host-provided. This pattern does NOT bundle any photography — pattern ships only the layout + responsive contract. Hosts must source their own licensed brand photography. The specimen uses CSS-generated gradient placeholders labelled as fixtures to avoid licensing entanglement; production swaps in real sources.

ariaPatterns.ariaLabelledby: <section aria-labelledby='...'> points to the h1's id.
ariaPatterns.decorativeVsInformative: If the photo carries information (product shot, named team member, identifiable place), . If purely decorative (abstract texture, mood photography), (empty alt — NOT aria-hidden, alt='' tells AT to skip).

name: hero-photo-media
role: Wrapper for the responsive + image. Hosts the responsive sources, the LCP-optimized , and an optional overlay layer for text-on-image contrast. Manages object-fit, object-position, and aspect-ratio.

meta.objectFitPolicy: cover = fill the container, crop overflow. The default; ensures no awkward letter-boxing. contain = fit inside container, may letterbox.
meta.focalPointPolicy: object-position controls which part of the image is preserved when cropping. focal-point='top' = preserve top of image; 'bottom-left' = preserve bottom-left. Critical for portraits where the face must stay in frame.

ariaPatterns.imgAltDiscipline: alt='' for purely decorative photography. alt='descriptive-text' for informative photography. Never aria-hidden on (use alt='' instead — alt='' is the correct way to mark a decorative image).

name: hero-photo-overlay
role: Optional overlay layer applied to the image for text-on-image contrast. Four registers — none (no overlay), gradient-from-bottom (dark fade from bottom), gradient-from-side (dark fade from text-anchored side), solid-tint (full coverage tint at low opacity). Always aria-hidden — purely decorative contrast aid.

registers.none: No overlay. Used when image natively has enough contrast — light-on-dark or dark-on-light scenes.
registers.gradient-from-bottom: Linear gradient from transparent (top) to dark (bottom). Used when text sits at the bottom of the image (image-full-bleed layout).
registers.gradient-from-side: Linear gradient from transparent (image side) to dark (text side). Used for image-right/image-left layouts when text needs extra contrast at the image-text boundary.
registers.solid-tint: Full-coverage tint at low opacity. Used as a fallback when the image is too busy for a directional gradient.

tones.dark: oklch(0% 0 0) — pure black. Default. Text on top reads as --text-on-accent (white).
tones.light: oklch(100% 0 0) — pure white. Used on dark photography. Text on top reads as --text-emphasis (near-black).
tones.accent: Theme accent. Used to color-anchor the hero to the brand identity.

meta.alwaysAriaHidden: Always aria-hidden='true'. The overlay is decorative contrast aid; the photo's semantic meaning lives in the .

name: hero-photo-eyebrow
role: Small uppercase pre-heading above the headline. Same anatomy as hero-type-only's hero-eyebrow. On image-overlay / image-full-bleed layouts, color shifts to --text-on-accent (light) for legibility against the photographed background.

meta.toneOnImage: On image-overlay / image-full-bleed layouts, prefer tone='on-image' (light against dark overlay).

name: hero-photo-headline
role: The display headline. h1 by default. Color shifts to --text-on-accent on image-overlay layouts.

meta.textShadowOnImage: On image-overlay without a full-coverage tint, an optional text-shadow can guarantee contrast: text-shadow: 0 1px 2px oklch(0% 0 0 / 0.4). Used sparingly — prefer overlay or tint.

name: hero-photo-subhead
role: Supporting subtitle. Same contract as hero-type-only's hero-subhead. Color shifts on image-overlay layouts to maintain contrast.

name: hero-photo-actions
role: CTA cluster composing button-system. On image-overlay layouts, ghost-intent CTAs receive a soft semi-transparent surface + backdrop-blur for legibility against unpredictable image content (same technique as hero-gradient-mesh).

meta.ghostOnImage: On image-overlay, ghost CTA gets backdrop-filter: blur(8px) + color-mix surface for legibility.

name: hero-photo-meta
role: Optional small-print row. Same 3 registers as hero-type-only (disclaimer / social-proof / trust-badges). Color shifts on image-overlay layouts.

meta.trustBadgeAltText: Trust-badge images carry alt-text naming the certification (per hero-type-only contract).
